commit.message:
fix: normalize image media_type for Claude API compatibility

Claude API only accepts image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp.
Mac screenshots and some dragged images can have unsupported types
(heic, bmp, tiff, svg). Frontend now converts unsupported formats
to PNG via canvas. Backend also validates media_type as safety net.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add polls support: create, vote, end, inline bar charts (#110)

* Add polls support: create, vote, end, inline bar charts (#64)

Implement full Signal polls feature covering the complete lifecycle:

- Parse incoming pollCreate/pollVote/pollTerminate from signal-cli
- Send polls via sendPollCreate/sendPollVote/sendPollTerminate RPC
- Store poll data and votes in SQLite (schema v11)
- /poll command: `/poll "question" "opt1" "opt2" [--single]`
- Inline bar chart rendering with vote counts and percentages
- Vote overlay (action menu → Vote) with j/k/Space/Enter navigation
- End Poll action for poll creators
- Sync support for polls sent from other devices

Closes #64

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix poll review issues: dedup poll_data, race condition, text truncation

- Remove duplicate PollData construction in /poll handler; clone before
  move instead of rebuilding from scratch
- Handle race condition in handle_poll_created where poll event arrives
  before the message — insert a placeholder DisplayMessage so the poll
  is always visible
- Truncate long poll option labels to 11 chars + ellipsis in bar chart
  to maintain alignment (char-safe for multibyte)
- Eliminate redundant options.clone() in vote action handler by saving
  len() before move

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix poll race condition: buffer deferred poll data instead of placeholder

The placeholder approach in c08c296 would cause duplicate messages when
the poll event arrived before the message, since handle_message doesn't
deduplicate by timestamp. Instead, buffer orphaned PollData in a HashMap
and attach it when the real message arrives via handle_message.

Keeps the other review fixes from c08c296 (dedup poll_data construction,
redundant clone removal, option label truncation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

fix: super admins bypass plan checks for invites (#93)

* fix: eliminate all visual jank sources in GTM Kanban board

- Remove transition: 'all .15s' from task cards (caused unexpected
  visual effects on any CSS property change)
- Replace transition: 'all .2s' with targeted transitions on filter
  buttons and user view tabs
- Replace direct DOM style mutations in drag handlers with React
  state (dragOverCol) for proper reconciliation
- Clean up onDragEnd to reset both dragId and dragOverCol
- Add useCallback import for future optimization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove autosave debounce, drag-and-drop, and card hover jank from GTM board

- Replace scheduleSave (600ms debounce timer) with immediate saveBoard
- Remove all drag-and-drop (dragId, dragOverCol, onDragStart, onDragEnd, onDragOver, onDragLeave, onDrop, draggable)
- Remove className="card" from MRR progress bar to eliminate CSS hover transitions
- Tasks now moved only via arrow buttons, saves happen instantly on action

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: wrap long notes/titles in GTM task cards with word-break

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: constrain GTM grid columns so long text wraps instead of overflowing

Add minWidth: 0 and overflow: hidden to column containers so CSS grid
columns respect 1fr width instead of expanding to fit content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: allow super admins to invite team members

- Frontend: treat app_role='admin' as team owner in TeamsPage so they
  can see the Settings tab and use the invite UI
- Backend: invite-member edge function now checks caller's app_role
  and allows admins to bypass the team.created_by ownership check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: check team owner's plan (not caller's) when admin sends invite

The plan/billing check used userId (the caller) to look up the plan,
but when a super admin sends the invite, their profile has no paid plan.
Now uses team.created_by to check the actual team owner's plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error message in invite-member for debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error on invite failure instead of generic message

Catch network/CORS errors separately, handle non-JSON responses,
and show HTTP status code when error body is empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: disable gateway JWT verification for invite-member edge function

The Supabase gateway was rejecting requests with 401 before our
function code ran, returning non-JSON without CORS headers. Our
function already handles JWT verification internally (lines 40-57),
so gateway verification is redundant and prevents proper error responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: super admins bypass plan/billing and member limit checks for invites

Super admins (app_role='admin') now skip all plan, billing, and member
count checks when sending invites. Previously failed because admin
plan is not in PLAN_MAX_MEMBERS map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix pop on stop: clear decay timers before fade-out ramp

Decay tremolo setInterval could fire during fade-out and cancel
the ramp via cancelScheduledValues, causing an abrupt cutoff.
Now timers are cleared immediately and _isPlaying set to false
before scheduling the fade-out.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: jersey name identification and block spurious club tags (#22)

* fix: script bugs in image pipeline (diacritics, skip logic, step order)

- scrape_images: add missing ú/ó/í diacritics to _tm_slug() so Torrente
  and Córdoba get Transfermarkt photos
- generate_embeddings: fix _should_skip always returning False by tracking
  disk_image_count separately from num_images (embedded count)
- auto_update: run image audit before embedding generation to avoid
  computing embeddings twice (was: embed → audit → re-embed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add difficult-condition image source (rain, snow, fog, partial face)

Add source 9 (fetch_difficult_condition_photos) to the image scraping
pipeline, targeting photos in adverse conditions: rain, snow, fog, night
matches, partial face visibility, side profiles, and distant shots.

- Increase DEFAULT_TARGET from 60 to 85 (25 new difficult-condition images)
- 11 Bing queries per player covering weather, occlusion, and angle variety
- Sources 1-8 fill first ~60 slots, source 9 fills remaining ~25
- Same Bing scraping pattern as fetch_action_photos (rate limiting, dedup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: increase auto_update timeout from 1h to 90min in handler.py

With 9 image sources (including the new difficult-conditions source),
the full pipeline (scrape 29 players + audit + 5-model embeddings +
archive export/upload) exceeds the previous 1-hour limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: skip headshot sources when player already has 55+ images

When a player already has 55+ clean images from sources 1-8, skip
those sources entirely and only try the difficult-conditions source.
This avoids hundreds of wasted HTTP requests that return only
duplicates, reducing scraping time from 30+ min to ~5 min.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use GPU (cuda) instead of CPU for audit and embedding scripts

Both audit_images.py and generate_embeddings.py were hardcoded to
device="cpu", causing them to run on CPU even on RunPod GPU workers.
This made each script take 25-30 min instead of ~5 min on GPU.

Now defaults to "cuda" via ML_DEVICE env var (falls back to CPU if
GPU unavailable via onnxruntime's provider fallback).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect new images in _all_embeddings_current() pre-check

Without this, the lightweight pre-check in auto_update.py would return
True when all players have recent embeddings (last_updated < 7 days),
even after scraping added new images. This caused generate_embeddings.py
to be skipped entirely — new images never got embedded.

Now checks disk_image_count against actual file count per player.
Also returns False when disk_image_count field is missing (old archives).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auto-detect GPU availability instead of defaulting to cuda

Uses torch.cuda.is_available() as default instead of hardcoded "cuda".
ML_DEVICE env var still overrides if set. Prevents crashes on CPU-only
environments (VPS) while still using GPU on RunPod.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: expose PRECOMPUTED_ARCHIVE_URL as runtime ENV and merge auto-update output streams

The archive upload to S3 was silently failing because PRECOMPUTED_ARCHIVE_URL
was only a Docker build ARG, not available at runtime. handler.py couldn't
derive ML_ARCHIVE_UPLOAD_URL, so auto_update.py fell back to the unreachable
default http://dam-api:8080 address.

Also merges stdout+stderr in auto_update subprocess so loguru output
(which goes to stderr) is captured in output_tail instead of being lost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: accept all image formats, improve quality, fix false club detection, speed up analysis

Upload:
- Accept AVIF, HEIC, HEIF, SVG, GIF, TIFF, BMP, PDF, video formats
- Magic byte validation for all new formats (ISOBMFF, SVG, GIF, BMP, TIFF, PDF)
- Streamlined perceptual hash to avoid blocking upload on non-seekable streams

Image quality:
- Explicit JPEG quality per variant (80-92) instead of default (~75)
- Lanczos3 resampler for all resizes (sharpest downscaling)

Club detection:
- Raised SigLIP team threshold from 0.5 to 0.65 to reduce false positives
- Removed green jersey from opponent labels (conflicts with Dinamo GK)
- Improved Dinamo label specificity (blue and white striped)

Analysis speed:
- Start sponsor + stadium detection in parallel with jersey OCR/face recognition
  instead of waiting for player identification to complete first
- Saves ~2-3s per analysis (sponsor Gemini calls overlap with face pipeline)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: update validator tests for expanded file type support

Tests now reflect that GIF, BMP, TIFF, AVIF, HEIC, HEIF, SVG, MP4,
MOV, WebM, and PDF are valid upload types. Invalid type tests use
actually-unsupported types like octet-stream, zip, audio.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: exclude youth players (Rajic, Ruskovacki) from roster scraping

Transfermarkt returns 31 players including U19/youth players who appear
on the first team page. Added EXCLUDED_PLAYERS set to filter them out
during roster merge. Excluded players will be deactivated if already
in the database.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: increase server resource limits and fix Kestrel upload size limit

Server has 11GB RAM but Docker containers were limited for 2GB — causing
slow page loads and failed analyses. Raise all container memory limits to
use ~8GB total. Also fix Kestrel's 128MB multipart body limit that blocked
RunPod archive uploads (1.16GB).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: jersey name identification and block descriptive club tags

Player identification now works when face is not visible:
- Fast-path jersey OCR runs on ALL detections (not just Dinamo-classified)
  so GKs in yellow/different jerseys get identified via number or name
- Jersey back text (surname) is now a PRIMARY identification method —
  "LIVAKOVIC" on the back directly maps to the player
- Lower face threshold for non-Dinamo from 0.60 to 0.50

Block spurious club tags:
- Add "team-interaction" and other compound tags to blocklist
- When no calendar data, only accept known clubs (prevents hallucinated
  clubs like "team-interaction" appearing as Club entities)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix decay tremolo pop: smooth 5ms attack ramp instead of instant jump to 1

The setInterval callback was snapping gain from ~0 to 1 instantly,
causing audible clicks. Now ramps smoothly over 5ms before decay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remaining SRE availability findings — batch 2 (#18)

## Summary

Addresses the remaining 16 SRE availability findings from the
operational review (batch 1 was merged in #17).

- **H3**: Bounded DynamoDB pagination (`max_items` param) +
`Select='COUNT'` for `summary()` to prevent OOM on large datasets
- **M2**: Idempotent `put_event` — unique suffix generated before retry
loop to prevent duplicate audit records
- **M7**: Removed stale `tag-index` GSI reference from StateStore
docstring
- **H1**: CloudWatch alarms for DynamoDB throttling and system errors +
SNS topic
- **H4**: KMS key policy denying `ScheduleKeyDeletion` and `DisableKey`
- **M1**: S3 noncurrent version expiration (365 days) to prevent
unbounded Glacier growth
- **H5**: CDK synth validation job added to CI
- **L4**: `fail-fast: false` on test matrix
- **L5**: Coverage HTML artifact generation
- **M8**: Tag vs pyproject.toml version validation in publish workflow
- **L2**: Pre-commit ruff version aligned to v0.15.4

**Risk accepted/deferred** (with rationale): H2 (S3 CRR), M3 (rotate-key
resume), M5 (encrypt retry scope), M6 (DynamoDB exports), L1 (CDK
floating versions)

## Test plan
- [x] 78/78 unit tests pass
- [x] ruff check clean
- [x] ruff format clean
- [x] mypy strict clean
- [x] cdk synth --strict passes
- [ ] CI green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
